creative.concept: The 3× gap — stat card revealing how much more people speak than type every day, and the ~11,000 words that vanish. First use of an external universal-behavior stat (vs. Jot's own builder numbers). Targets the Saturday lifestyle + productivity crowd; red '3×' is the scroll-stopper.

cards.title: 3× — more words spoken than typed every day
cards.use: Primary visual for LinkedIn, X, and Instagram today. The red '3×' stops the scroll; '~11,000 words / day Lost' in red is the punchline. Export as 1080×1080 PNG.

storyboard.title: Saturday to-do list — spoken in 12 seconds
storyboard.use: Instagram Reel / X video / GIF — weekend personal use case; fresh after a week of developer-focused clips; relatable for a broader Saturday audience

shots.visual: Reminders app open on Mac, empty list under 'Saturday' header, cursor blinking — blank page anxiety
Hand presses ⌥ Space; Dynamic Island-style pill appears pulsing under Mac notch
Pill shows pulsing audio waveform; user speaks five tasks aloud at a natural pace
All five tasks appear in Reminders list; pill vanishes; list complete
Wide Mac desktop; completed Reminders list visible; jot-transcribe.com watermark lower-right

drafts.text:
You speak ~16,000 words a day. You type ~5,000.

That's 11,000 words that vanish — never emailed, never written down.

I built Jot to capture them. Press a hotkey, speak, the words land wherever your cursor is. On-device, nothing leaves your Mac.

72 days. 231 downloads. The gap is real.

What would you write if typing wasn't the bottleneck?

#macOS #productivity #indiedev
drafts.note: Attach 3x-stat-gap.svg (export as 1080×1080 PNG). Do NOT put the link in the post body — LinkedIn downranks posts with external links. Paste the utm_url as the FIRST COMMENT immediately after posting. Closing question is the engagement trigger — answer every reply.

drafts.text:
You speak ~16,000 words/day. You type ~5,000. That's 11,000 words that never get written.

Jot: hotkey → speak → text at cursor. On-device. Free.
drafts.note: Attach 3x-stat-gap.svg (export as PNG). No link in post body — post the utm_url as the first reply immediately after posting. ~148 characters, within the 200-char limit.

drafts.text:
I tracked how many words I speak vs. type on a typical workday — and built a free dictation app to close the gap (I made this)

The average knowledge worker speaks around 16,000 words a day but types only ~5,000. That's 3× more spoken — roughly 11,000 words per day that just don't get written down.

So I built Jot. Press a hotkey, speak, and the transcript pastes wherever your cursor is in any app — email, Slack, terminal, your code editor. No window switching.

Transcription runs entirely on-device on the Apple Neural Engine (Parakeet TDT model). No audio ever leaves the Mac. Works offline. No account, no subscription, no telemetry.

Some patterns after 72 days of using it myself:
- Morning Slack standup: 90 seconds of typing → 20 seconds spoken
- Commit messages: I actually describe what I changed now instead of writing "fix bug"
- PR descriptions: 30-second verbal explanation beats 10 minutes of typed bullets
- Email backlog on a busy afternoon: batch and speak — 5 replies in 3 minutes

The thing I didn't expect: dictating feels more natural for explanations. When I type, I compress. When I speak, I give context. Reviewers and teammates have noticed.

Apple Silicon only, macOS 15+. Free and open source.

(I built this — disclosure)

Link in comments.

opportunities.title: 11 Best Superwhisper Alternatives in 2026 — Voibe
opportunities.why: High-SEO roundup that excludes Jot — the only fully free, open-source on-device option. Previously flagged; if the comment hasn't been posted yet, this is a live opportunity with high-intent readers.
opportunities.suggested_reply: Great list. One worth adding: Jot (jot-transcribe.com) — the only fully free, open-source, on-device option. Press a hotkey, speak, text lands at your cursor in any app. Runs on the Apple Neural Engine — no cloud, no subscription, no audio leaves your Mac. 11 languages as of v1.16. I built it. (Disclosure: developer.)

opportunities.title: Best Speech-to-Text Tools for Mac in 2026 (What Reddit Actually Recommends) — Resonant
opportunities.why: Competitor article aggregating Reddit voice-rec recommendations. Jot almost certainly absent. High-SEO page that shapes what comparison-shoppers find. Site returned 403 on fetch — verify reachability before outreach Jul 1.

opportunities.title: Wispr Flow vs Superwhisper vs MacWhisper (2026) — Spokenly
opportunities.why: Three-way paid comparison ($180/yr vs $249/yr vs €59 one-time) that omits the $0 open-source option entirely. Reaches users already frustrated by dictation pricing. Not comment-enabled — flag for outreach July 1.

notes: Downloads held at 231 today (delta 0) — typical Saturday pattern, consistent with prior weekend plateaus (Jun 20–21). avg7 = 11/day. The 250-download milestone is 19 downloads away (~2 days at current pace). iTunes API returned 403 for the 15th consecutive day; appstore.json date updated to 2026-06-27, all values preserved including installs: 0 from App Store Connect. Outreach window remains closed (last outreach June 24; window reopens July 1 — priority targets: machow2.com, speakmac.app, Resonant's 'What Reddit Recommends' page, Voibe comment). Reddit recommended:true today (last true June 23, 4 days ago) — r/macapps. Instagram recommended:false (last true June 26, 1 day ago; earliest June 29). Today's stat card breaks new ground: previous stat cards used Jot's own builder numbers (downloads, releases, days); this uses a universal human-behavior fact that works as a standalone insight regardless of whether the reader knows Jot. Format 'surprising stat' last used Jun 17 (10 days ago). No carousel today (last ran June 25 = 2 days ago; next eligible June 29+). Jot confirmed absent from all Mac dictation roundups surfaced by search: Voibe, Resonant, Spokenly, Ryan Shrott Medium, machow2.com, speakmac.app, Blazing Fast Transcription roundup, Granola transcription guide — none mention Jot. The comparison-table Reddit draft from June 26 is eligible to post today and is an alternative to today's stat-angle post.
